title: The Tea Party Opens
description: On pages 19–21, this part focuses on Nami, Brook, Reiju, Sanji, Luffy as The wedding ceremony and tea party begin, with Big Mom’s enemies preparing to strike.
scene: The wedding begins under Big Mom’s watch, with hidden assassination plans moving beneath the ceremony.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Today, I'm going to stop being straw hat luffy

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: And instead be Samurai helmet Luffy!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: doesn't really roll off the tongue, in my opinion.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You sound like some sort of action figure. Don't do it.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't do it because it makes me sound like an action figure?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's like saying "Don't do it cause it makes you sound super cool!"

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: The treasure room.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: Why do you stand again...

dialogue.speaker: Brook
dialogue.text: Soul King?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Do you really want a copy of this slab of stone more than you want Sanji back?!

dialogue.speaker: Reiju
dialogue.text: ...Yes. Sanji is a kind man.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: FALTER...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's why I suspected that he would never return to us.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I have no idea what I kind of trap to you all may have waiting for us all...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But because of how incredibly kind-hearted he is...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We'll refuse to budge once he's decided to sacrifice himself for the sake of others!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: On the other hand, we also have a captain who breaks through any and all walls in order to pursue his beliefs and ambitions.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How things end up with Sanji... will be decided solely amongst those two!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: Hahahaha... I see!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Not quite! Even if, in the worst case scenario, Sanji decides to never return to us...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We would never want him to feel a single drop of guilt.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And so in order to be able to show him that we've gained something amazing out of this trip, even if we don't end up leaving with him...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I will be completing my task ...of getting away with this road poneglyph!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Mamama... your so-called "worst case scenario" ain't even that bad!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If Sanji decides to never return?! That's your worst case scenario?! Are you sure nobody is going to end up getting killed on this trip?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: I have a few more

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Heck, everyone you cherish might end up dying because of this journey!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: Young lady... What kind of fool...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: Would ever plan to die?!

summary: This section advances the Whole Cake Island — Tea Party Begins storyline with 29 dialogue beats. The wedding and tea party begin, placing Sanji, Pudding, Big Mom, Bege and the Straw Hats on a collision course.
